name: Town Forge

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
**Town Forge 1.1.1**

Ô£¿ New

Map from note Properties ÔÇö keep a map's settings in the note's Properties instead of the code block. Prefix any key with townforge- (e.g. townforge-terrain, townforge-seed, townforge-settlement) and leave the block empty; the map reads them from the note it's in. List properties become letter runs (townforge-edges: [N, E] ÔåÆ NE) and checkbox properties work as on/off.
Draw a map from another note ÔÇö put from: [[Some Town]] in a town-forge block to render a map from that note's Properties, no copying. Explicit lines in the block still override.

­ƒöº Improved

Set-up readiness checklist on Create place templates ÔÇö a live checklist (Randomness, Templater trigger, Fantasy Portrait Pack, Fantasy Hub content) that turns green as each piece is in place and only enables the button when the generators are actually detected.
Preview panel action bar ÔÇö the Generate, Copy/Insert/Save, and Export buttons now sit in a sticky footer that stays visible at any panel width, with wrapping rows so nothing gets cut off. No more resizing to reach Export.
Documented the new Properties feature in the README and corrected the farms/forest range (0ÔÇô4).

­ƒÉø Fixed

Create place templates no longer sets off Templater prompts. The template folder is now added to Templater's excluded folders automatically, so Trigger on new file creation won't execute the template files as they're written (which caused "Settlement size?" / "Town name?" prompts and could overwrite the templates). Stamped place notes still run normally.
Correctly detect Templater's Trigger on new file creation toggle (it's a per-device setting), so the checklist reflects it accurately.

Full changelog: https://github.com/Obsidian-TTRPG-Community/Town-Forge/compare/1.0.4...1.1.1

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description: Map from note Properties (townforge-* keys and from: [[Note]]), plus a setup readiness checklist for Create place templates. Closes #1.

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
> ÔÜá´©Å Beta pre-release for testing. Install via BRAT (Add beta plugin ÔåÆ `Obsidian-TTRPG-Community/Town-Forge`). Not yet served to the community store.

Town Forge 1.0.4 deepens the integration with **TTRPG Tools - Maps**, **Randomness**, and **Heraldry Weaver** so a generated town comes with matching map pins, icons, and self-populating place notes.

### New
- **One-click "Send pin icons to TTRPG Tools - Maps"** button. Detects when the Maps plugin is installed and writes Town Forge's 15 pin icons straight into its icon library ÔÇö every pin shows the right glyph, no manual icon setup. Safe to re-run; it only adds/updates its own icons.
- **Bundled place templates + pins.** The "Create / update" button seeds Town Forge's own place-note templates, and the package now ships a matching pin set so pins, templates, and icons all line up.
- **"Pre-made map content" setup section** that groups the template and icon steps with a short explanation of how they work together.

### Improved
- **Export progress feedback** ÔÇö the "Export to TTRPG Tools: Maps" button now shows a busy state and a notice while it works, so long exports don't look frozen.
- **Ecosystem quick-links** (Randomness, Templater, Heraldry Weaver, ITS theme) on the templates row for one-click install of the companions.
- Templates and setup guide refreshed for **Randomness 1.1.0** (NPC portraits).

### Requires
- **Randomness** (with its Fantasy Hub content + Fantasy Portrait Pack) for named, portraited NPCs
- **Templater** with "Trigger on new file creation" for the place-note content
- **TTRPG Tools - Maps** to render the interactive map and pins
releases.name: Town Forge 1.0.4

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
# Town Forge 1.0.3

Two improvements since 1.0.0: a new `size` property on exported place notes, and a meaningful rework of how roads and towns relate on river maps.

## What's new

- **Settlement size in frontmatter** ÔÇö each exported place note now carries a `size:` property set to the settlement's size (one of `hamlet`, `village`, `small_town`, `town`, `large_town`, `small_city`, `city`, `large_city`, `metropolis`). A new `{{size}}` template token is also available, so a Templater body can read `fm.size` and pass it to a size-aware Randomness table (e.g. banded shop stock).
- **River-aware road approaches** ÔÇö `auto` edges now guarantee approach roads on both banks for river maps. Bridge rate across river towns improved from ~60% to ~74%.
- **Town built on the arterial** ÔÇö the town centre is now placed *on* the road network by construction (snapped to the nearest arterial or the bridge centre), rather than computed independently. River towns now reliably gather around their road or bridge instead of drifting off into empty terrain. 100% of river towns now sit on the road network (median 0px, max ~1px).

## Install

**Manual:** download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `versions.json` below into `.obsidian/plugins/town-forge/`, then enable under Settings ÔåÆ Community Plugins.  
**BRAT:** add this repo as a beta plugin (or update if already added).

## Notes

- No breaking changes; existing maps regenerate identically by seed for non-river terrains.
- For river terrains, regenerating an existing seed will produce a different-looking map (the road and town logic changed by design).
releases.name: 1.0.3 - Two improvements since 1.0.0: a new `size` property on exported place notes, and a meaningful rework of how roads and towns relate on river maps.

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
# Town Forge 1.0.1

Adds the settlement **size** to exported place notes, so templates and Randomness rolls can scale their content to the settlement.

## What's new
- **Settlement size in frontmatter** ÔÇö each exported place note now carries a `size:` property set to the settlement's size (one of `hamlet`, `village`, `small_town`, `town`, `large_town`, `small_city`, `city`, `large_city`, `metropolis`).
- **`{{size}}` template token** ÔÇö use it in your place templates; a Templater body can read `fm.size` and pass it to a size-aware table (e.g. banded shop stock).

## Notes
- Place notes are written during a TTRPG Tools: Maps export (full-mode maps).
- See [docs/randomness-templater-setup.md](docs/randomness-templater-setup.md) for wiring `size` (and `subtype`) into Randomness + Templater.

## Install
Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `versions.json` below into `.obsidian/plugins/town-forge/`, then enable under Settings ÔåÆ Community Plugins.
releases.name: 1.0.1 - Add settlement size to place-note frontmatter (v1.0.1)

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
# Town Forge 1.0.0 ÔÇö First Public Release

Procedurally generate fantasy landscape and settlement maps for tabletop RPGs ÔÇö terrain, forests, mountains, roads, and settlements from a hamlet to a metropolis, with a live preview panel and optional pinned-map export.

## Highlights
- **Procedural maps** ÔÇö river, coastal, lake, mountain, and inland terrain; settlements from hamlet to metropolis with organic streets, walls, bridges, and landmarks (castle, temple, market, barracks, docks, mills, stables).
- **Isolated tower** ÔÇö a rare wizard/necromancer's tower placed out by a forest or mountain edge, with its own path back to the road.
- **Live preview panel** ÔÇö adjust terrain, size, roads, density, and landmarks and regenerate as you go.
- **Optional TTRPG Tools: Maps export** (off by default) ÔÇö interactive pinned maps plus one note per place, generated from your own templates. Configurable pin types, names, subtypes, icons, and layers.
- **Deterministic** ÔÇö the same seed always produces the same map.

## Install
**Manual:** download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `versions.json` below into `.obsidian/plugins/town-forge/`, then enable under Settings ÔåÆ Community Plugins.
**BRAT:** add this repo as a beta plugin.

## Thanks
- **RedReaper21** ÔÇö for extensive help testing and tuning the plugin.
- **Jareika** ÔÇö for [TTRPG Tools: Maps](https://github.com/Jareika/zoom-map), the plugin Town Forge exports its interactive maps to.
- **[Randomness](https://github.com/Obsidian-TTRPG-Community/Randomness)** ÔÇö drives correlated place names and note bodies from templates.
releases.name: 1.0.0 - docs: prep first public release (v1.0.0), README acknowledgements, versions.json
